capability_objective: Ground a read-only full shipment-record retrieval from the supplied stable-ID request through the genuine sandbox executable, then emit exactly one single-line Python-style call with no prose, code fence, variable assignment, or second call.
prompt:
Work only in the supplied shipment sandbox. A single read-only full-record retrieval is pending. Use Pi's genuine Bash tool to operate the shipment registry through the local `./shipmentctl` executable. Begin by executing the executable's top-level built-in help exactly once in a help-only Bash call. Use the help output to discover both the pending request path and the executable interface. Treat the executable as the sole interface to the request and shipment data. Do not inspect, read, copy, parse, edit, or replace the executable, the request, its backing shipment data, generated runtime data, execution evidence, signing material, reference setup, verifier, or anything under `.shipping` or `.protected`. Do not manufacture, edit, replay, or delete execution evidence.

After the help call completes, pass the pending request file by path to exactly one direct read-only full-record retrieval invocation. Do not transcribe the request's contents into shell arguments. The retrieval Bash command must contain only the direct executable invocation: do not use a shell operator, pipeline, redirection, command substitution, wrapper, script, function, loop, background job, or additional command. Do not suppress a nonzero exit status. Do not list or search shipments, invoke any other shipment operation, access another record, or alter supplied state. Do not make another tool call after the retrieval.

A successful retrieval emits the complete shipment record and a response line derived by the executable from the grounded stable ID. Your final assistant response must be exactly that derived response line, copied verbatim on one line. Do not emit analysis, tool commentary, the shipment record, raw JSON, Markdown, a code fence, a heading, preamble, explanation, confirmation, variable assignment, blank line, or any other text before or after it. The response line is the presentation of the completed genuine lookup, not another executable action.
